reasoning: Tapestry (TPR) recently dropped significantly on its Nov 6 earnings due to soft holiday guidance, but bounced back rapidly from an intraday low of 94.60 to close at 103.04 on Nov 7 (which serves as the baseline for this question). Despite the earnings drop, the company raised its full-year EPS guidance and announced a new $1 billion share repurchase program (approximately 4.4% of its market cap), providing solid buying pressure. Analysts maintain a strong consensus price target of roughly $123.85, indicating the stock is likely undervalued at 103.04. While an ex-dividend date on Dec 5 will subtract $0.40 from the share price, the strong post-earnings bounce back and buyback dynamics warrant a higher-than-average short-term probability of being above the 103.04 baseline. However, in accordance with the instructions, uncertainty must increase with the forecast horizon, leading to probabilities closer to 0.5 for the more distant dates of December and February.
